title: The Two Marks on Jesus’ Palms
summary: There are two cuttings or marks on the palms of the hands of the Lord Jesus Christ. The first mark is the nail prints that we inflicted upon the Lord collectively on the Cross at Golgotha. “Then saith he to Thomas, Reach hither thy finger, and behold my hands; and reach hither thy hand, and thrust it into my side: and be not faithless, but believing” (John 20:27). All of us are invited to examine this cut or piercing we inflicted upon the Lord. “But he was wounded for our transgressions, he was bruised for our iniquities: the chastisement of our peace was upon him; and with his stripes we are healed” (Isaiah 53:5). The Lord has refused to erase these wounds or scars from his hands because they bear witness of his Godhead, the expiation of our sins, his resurrection, his return, and his immeasurable love for humanity. This is the wound or the marking we inflicted upon him, but there is another mark on the palms of his hands that he himself inscribed or cut into. Isaiah 49:16 Behold, I have graven thee upon the palms of my hands; thy walls are continually before me. The Hebrew word ḥāqaq is translated as “to cut in or on, cut upon, engrave, inscribe” according to James Strong. Look at the palms of your hands. They contain lines that do not communicate anything intelligible, but the palms of the Lord Jesus’ hands proudly display your name and mine.
